---
description: "Internationalization (i18n) patterns for Next.js 16.1.6 App Router. Covers single-locale apps, DIY multi-locale, and next-intl integration — including proxy.ts ordering and cacheComponents compatibility."
applyTo: "app/[locale]/**, app/[lang]/**, i18n/**, messages/**, dictionaries/**"
---

# Internationalization (i18n) — Next.js 16.1.6

> **Source**: Next.js 16.1.6 official i18n guide — `nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/internationalization`

## Decision Matrix — Which Tier to Use

Pick the lowest tier that covers the project's requirements. Do not over-engineer.

| Tier                          | When                                                                  | What                                                                           |
| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Tier 1 — Single locale**    | App ships in one language only                                        | Use `APP_LOCALE` in `lib/constants.ts`. Nothing else needed.                   |
| **Tier 2 — DIY multi-locale** | ≤ 3 locales, no pluralization, no client-side locale switching        | Official Next.js pattern: `app/[lang]/`, `getDictionary()`, JSON files         |
| **Tier 3 — `next-intl`**      | ≥ 3 locales, pluralization, ICU messages, typed keys, locale switcher | `pnpm add next-intl` — wraps proxy.ts, provides Server + Client Component APIs |

**Default for this template**: Tier 1. Tier 2 or 3 must be added explicitly by the project.

---

## Tier 1 — Single Locale (Template Default)

The template ships with `APP_LOCALE = "en"` in `lib/constants.ts`. This value drives:

- `<html lang={APP_LOCALE}>` in `app/layout.tsx` and `app/global-error.tsx`
- `formatDate` / `formatDateTime` locale default in `lib/dates.ts`

**No routing changes needed.** Do not add `[lang]` or `[locale]` dynamic segments for single-locale apps.

When changing the app language, update `APP_LOCALE` and update `openGraph.locale` in
`app/layout.tsx` metadata (currently hardcoded as `"en_US"` — change to match your locale).

---

## Tier 2 — DIY Multi-Locale (Official Next.js Pattern)

Use when the project has 2–3 simple locales with no pluralization or client-side switching.

### Dependencies

```bash
pnpm add @formatjs/intl-localematcher negotiator
pnpm add -D @types/negotiator
```

### Directory structure

```
app/
├── [lang]/
│   ├── layout.tsx          # root layout with <html lang={lang}>
│   ├── page.tsx            # home
│   ├── dashboard/page.tsx  # all routes nested here
│   └── dictionaries/
│       ├── en.json
│       ├── es.json
│       └── nl.json
├── dictionaries.ts         # getDictionary(), hasLocale() — server-only
```

### `dictionaries.ts` — server-only locale loader

```ts
// app/[lang]/dictionaries.ts
import "server-only";

const dictionaries = {
  en: () => import("./dictionaries/en.json").then((m) => m.default),
  es: () => import("./dictionaries/es.json").then((m) => m.default),
};

export type Locale = keyof typeof dictionaries;

export const hasLocale = (locale: string): locale is Locale =>
  locale in dictionaries;

export const getDictionary = async (locale: Locale) => dictionaries[locale]();
```

### `app/[lang]/layout.tsx` — root layout with `generateStaticParams`

```tsx
// app/[lang]/layout.tsx
import { notFound } from "next/navigation";
import { hasLocale } from "./dictionaries";
import type { LayoutProps } from "next";

// Required with cacheComponents: true — empty array is a build error
export function generateStaticParams() {
  return [{ lang: "en" }, { lang: "es" }];
}

export default async function LocaleLayout({
  children,
  params,
}: Readonly<LayoutProps<"/[lang]">>) {
  const { lang } = await params;
  if (!hasLocale(lang)) notFound();

  return (
    <html lang={lang}>
      <body>{children}</body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

### `app/[lang]/page.tsx` — page with dictionary

```tsx
// app/[lang]/page.tsx
import { notFound } from "next/navigation";
import { getDictionary, hasLocale } from "./dictionaries";
import type { PageProps } from "next";

export default async function HomePage({
  params,
}: Readonly<PageProps<"/[lang]">>) {
  const { lang } = await params;
  if (!hasLocale(lang)) notFound();

  const dict = await getDictionary(lang);
  return <h1>{dict.home.title}</h1>;
}
```

### Update `proxy.ts` for locale detection

Add locale detection **before** the existing auth logic. The order matters:
locale redirect → auth rate limiter → auth session check.

```ts
// proxy.ts — add at the top, before the auth handler
import { match } from "@formatjs/intl-localematcher";
import Negotiator from "negotiator";

const SUPPORTED_LOCALES = ["en", "es"] as const;
const DEFAULT_LOCALE = "en";

function getLocale(request: NextRequest): string {
  const acceptLanguage =
    request.headers.get("accept-language") ?? DEFAULT_LOCALE;
  const headers = { "accept-language": acceptLanguage };
  const languages = new Negotiator({ headers }).languages();
  return match(languages, SUPPORTED_LOCALES, DEFAULT_LOCALE);
}

// Inside the auth handler, before auth checks:
const { pathname } = nextUrl;
const pathnameHasLocale = SUPPORTED_LOCALES.some(
  (locale) => pathname.startsWith(`/${locale}/`) || pathname === `/${locale}`,
);
if (!pathnameHasLocale) {
  const locale = getLocale(req);
  req.nextUrl.pathname = `/${locale}${pathname}`;
  return NextResponse.redirect(req.nextUrl);
}
```

### Update `ROUTES` for locale-prefixed paths

With `[lang]` routing, routes become `/en/dashboard`. Update `lib/constants.ts`:

```ts
// lib/constants.ts — locale-aware route helper
export function localeRoute(locale: string, path: string): string {
  return `/${locale}${path}`;
}

// Usage: localeRoute(lang, ROUTES.dashboard) → '/en/dashboard'
```

---

## Tier 3 — `next-intl` (Recommended for Complex i18n)

Use when: ≥ 3 locales, pluralization, ICU messages, typed translation keys, or a locale switcher component is needed.

### Installation

```bash
pnpm add next-intl
```

### Directory structure

```
app/
├── [locale]/
│   ├── layout.tsx          # NextIntlClientProvider, generateStaticParams
│   └── (all routes)
i18n/
│   ├── routing.ts          # defineRouting() — source of truth for supported locales
│   ├── request.ts          # getRequestConfig() — loads messages per request
│   └── navigation.ts       # createNavigation() — typed Link, useRouter, redirect
messages/
│   ├── en.json
│   └── es.json
```

### `i18n/routing.ts` — source of truth

```ts
// i18n/routing.ts
import { defineRouting } from "next-intl/routing";

export const routing = defineRouting({
  locales: ["en", "es", "fr"],
  defaultLocale: "en",
  // 'always' → /en/dashboard, /es/dashboard
  // 'as-needed' → /dashboard (default), /es/dashboard (non-default)
  localePrefix: "as-needed",
});

export type AppLocale = (typeof routing.locales)[number];
```

### `i18n/request.ts` — per-request config

```ts
// i18n/request.ts
import { getRequestConfig } from "next-intl/server";
import { routing } from "./routing";

export default getRequestConfig(async ({ requestLocale }) => {
  let locale = await requestLocale;
  if (!locale || !routing.locales.includes(locale as AppLocale)) {
    locale = routing.defaultLocale;
  }
  return {
    locale,
    messages: (await import(`../messages/${locale}.json`)).default,
  };
});
```

### `i18n/navigation.ts` — typed navigation helpers

```ts
// i18n/navigation.ts
import { createNavigation } from "next-intl/navigation";
import { routing } from "./routing";

// Use these instead of next/navigation in locale-aware routes
export const { Link, redirect, useRouter, usePathname, getPathname } =
  createNavigation(routing);
```

### `app/[locale]/layout.tsx`

```tsx
// app/[locale]/layout.tsx
import { NextIntlClientProvider } from "next-intl";
import { getMessages } from "next-intl/server";
import { notFound } from "next/navigation";
import { routing } from "@/i18n/routing";
import type { LayoutProps } from "next";

export function generateStaticParams() {
  // Required with cacheComponents: true — must return at least one locale
  return routing.locales.map((locale) => ({ locale }));
}

export default async function LocaleLayout({
  children,
  params,
}: Readonly<LayoutProps<"/[locale]">>) {
  const { locale } = await params;

  if (!routing.locales.includes(locale as AppLocale)) notFound();

  const messages = await getMessages();

  return (
    <html lang={locale}>
      <body>
        <NextIntlClientProvider messages={messages}>
          {children}
        </NextIntlClientProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

### `proxy.ts` integration — compose with `auth()`

Add next-intl's middleware as the first thing inside the auth handler:

```ts
// proxy.ts
import createIntlMiddleware from "next-intl/middleware";
import { routing } from "@/i18n/routing";

const intlMiddleware = createIntlMiddleware(routing);

export default auth((req) => {
  // 1. Locale detection and redirect — MUST run first so auth redirects
  //    land on the correct locale URL (e.g. /es/auth/sign-in, not /auth/sign-in).
  const { pathname } = req.nextUrl;
  const isApiRoute = pathname.startsWith("/api/");
  const isNextInternal = pathname.startsWith("/_next");

  if (!isApiRoute && !isNextInternal) {
    const intlResponse = intlMiddleware(req);
    if (intlResponse) return intlResponse;
  }

  // 2. CSP nonce + auth checks follow (existing logic unchanged)
  const nonce = Buffer.from(crypto.randomUUID()).toString("base64");
  // ... rest of existing proxy.ts
});
```

### Translation usage — Server Components

```tsx
// app/[locale]/dashboard/page.tsx
import { getTranslations } from "next-intl/server";

export default async function DashboardPage() {
  // Server-only — zero client bundle cost
  const t = await getTranslations("Dashboard");
  return <h1>{t("title")}</h1>;
}
```

### Translation usage — Client Components

```tsx
// components/locale-nav.tsx
"use client";
import { useTranslations } from "next-intl";

export function LocaleNav() {
  const t = useTranslations("Navigation");
  return <nav aria-label={t("ariaLabel")}>{/* ... */}</nav>;
}
```

### TypeScript — typed message keys

Create `global.d.ts` at the root to enable type-safe translation keys:

```ts
// global.d.ts (root of project)
import en from "./messages/en.json";

type Messages = typeof en;

declare global {
  // Use type safe message keys with `next-intl`
  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-empty-object-type
  interface IntlMessages extends Messages {}
}
```

With this in place, `t('Dashboard.title')` is type-checked — typos become compile errors.

---

## Common Rules (All Tiers)

- **`generateStaticParams` must return ≥ 1 locale** when `cacheComponents: true` — an empty array is a build error (see Common Pitfalls #12 in copilot-instructions.md)
- **Never put auth checks in `app/[locale]/layout.tsx`** — they can be bypassed; use `page.tsx` or `proxy.ts`
- **Locale redirect in `proxy.ts` runs before auth** — this ensures `/settings` → `/es/auth/sign-in` (not `/auth/sign-in`) when unauthenticated
- **`APP_LOCALE` in `lib/constants.ts` is for single-locale apps only** — for Tier 2/3, the `lang` or `locale` dynamic param replaces it as the source of truth; keep `APP_LOCALE` as the `defaultLocale` fallback
- **Do not hardcode `"en"` or `"en_US"` anywhere** — always derive from `APP_LOCALE` (Tier 1), `params.lang` / `params.locale` (Tier 2/3), or `routing.defaultLocale` (Tier 3)
- **Translation JSON files live in `messages/` (next-intl) or `dictionaries/` (DIY)** — choose one location and stay consistent
- **Mark `getDictionary` / `getMessages` files with `import 'server-only'`** — they should never run on the client

---

## Learnings
